title: Operating Engineers and Other Construction Equipment Operators
description: Operate one or several types of power construction equipment, such as motor graders, bulldozers, scrapers, compressors, pumps, derricks, shovels, tractors, or front-end loaders to excavate, move, and grade earth, erect structures, or pour concrete or other hard surface pavement. May repair and maintain equipment in addition to other duties.

tasks.text: Learn and follow safety regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take actions to avoid potential hazards or obstructions, such as utility lines, other equipment, other workers, or falling objects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start engines, move throttles, switches, or levers, or depress pedals to operate machines, such as bulldozers, trench excavators, road graders, or backhoes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate machine actions with other activities, positioning or moving loads in response to hand or audio signals from crew members.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Align machines, cutterheads, or depth gauge makers with reference stakes and guidelines or ground or position equipment, following hand signals of other workers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Locate underground services, such as pipes or wires, prior to beginning work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Signal operators to guide movement of tractor-drawn machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair and maintain equipment, making emergency adjustments or assisting with major repairs as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load and move dirt, rocks, equipment, or other materials, using trucks, crawler tractors, power cranes, shovels, graders, or related equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive and maneuver equipment equipped with blades in successive passes over working areas to remove topsoil, vegetation, or rocks or to distribute and level earth or terrain.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate tractors or bulldozers to perform such tasks as clearing land, mixing sludge, trimming backfills, or building roadways or parking lots.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor operations to ensure that health and safety standards are met.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Connect hydraulic hoses, belts, mechanical linkages, or power takeoff shafts to tractors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select and fasten bulldozer blades or other attachments to tractors, using hitches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate loaders to pull out stumps, rip asphalt or concrete, rough-grade properties, bury refuse, or perform general cleanup.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate equipment to demolish or remove debris or to remove snow from streets, roads, or parking lots.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep records of material or equipment usage or problems encountered.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Adjust handwheels and depress pedals to control attachments, such as blades, buckets, scrapers, or swing booms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check fuel supplies at sites to ensure adequate availability.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Talk to clients and study instructions, plans, or diagrams to establish work requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Drive tractor-trailer trucks to move equipment from site to site.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Push other equipment when extra traction or assistance is required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate road watering, oiling, or rolling equipment, or street sealing equipment, such as chip spreaders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate compactors, scrapers, or rollers to level, compact, or cover refuse at disposal grounds.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test atmosphere for adequate oxygen or explosive conditions when working in confined spaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn valves to control air or water output of compressors or pumps.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
